title: Ambrost Mugland
summary: A hidden Alkenstar power broker whose influence runs through the city's money, muscle, crooked institutions, and multiple player-character storylines.
concept: Crime financier and system-level operator at the center of the campaign's corruption web.
role: high-level power broker / criminal financier / hidden beneficiary

background.history: Current repo materials frame Mugland as a city-scale force whose reach extends through finance, muscle, and compromised institutions. Player-character files tie him to hidden trafficking, a wider criminal network, the fungal trade, and suspicious financial webs.
background.reasonInStory: He is one of the campaign's main pressure sources and a likely source or beneficiary of multiple active threads.

currentSituation.overview: Mugland remains an off-screen but central force shaping heists, investigations, reprisals, and underworld maneuvering in Alkenstar.
currentSituation.currentProblem: Multiple player characters are now converging on different parts of the network around him, including trafficking, fungus distribution, corrupt law, and ledgers.
currentSituation.currentLeverage: Money, a vault-backed business presence, criminal reach, and ties into crooked institutions.

motivations.shortTerm: Protect his position and keep sensitive operations buffered behind respectable fronts and compromised officials.
motivations.midTerm: Maintain control of the city's pressure points in money, muscle, and hidden trade.
motivations.longTerm: Remain the hidden beneficiary of Alkenstar's corrupt machinery.

presentation.behavior: Indirect, insulated, and difficult to reach directly.

gmUse.sceneFunction: Primary off-screen campaign antagonist, patron-risk, and convergence point for multiple investigations.
gmUse.whatTheyKnow: Potentially knows the truth behind the missing gifted children, key criminal-financial links, and parts of the fungal trade.
gmUse.howToEscalate: Have Mugland apply financial pressure, use institutional allies, close access routes, or retaliate through intermediaries.
gmUse.fallbackUse: Use his name, orders, vault access, or network traces to tie separate jobs into one larger structure.

campaignFitSummary: Central to the campaign's corruption, trafficking, financial, and underworld pressures. Best used as the hidden center of heists, investigations, reprisals, and network-reveal scenes.
notes: Built from current repo-backed campaign summaries plus player-character references. Exact appearance, ancestry, and location are not yet established in the subtree.

relations.note: Charles Greyland believes Mugland knows what happened to the missing gifted children.
Durog's notes place fungal trade activity within or near Mugland's network.
Flint's casework tied Mugland to a larger criminal and institutional network.
Zinriss is positioned to uncover the money trails around Mugland-linked trade.
